record_identity.title: Undisclosed agentic workspace replication and data-egress authority collapse

summary: An AI agent, coding assistant, connector, repository integration, or tool-enabled runtime copies, transmits, persists, indexes, or exports workspace data beyond the minimum material required for the requested operation without explicit, legible, purpose-bound, and appropriately scoped authority.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Authority to inspect a workspace does not imply authority to replicate, transmit, retain, train on, or otherwise reuse the workspace. Collapsing these permissions converts ordinary tool access into an undisclosed data-egress pathway, exposes source code, secrets, personal data, deleted history, and institutional records, and prevents users from understanding which material left the governed environment, why it was transferred, where it was stored, how long it will persist, and whether deletion or secondary-use controls apply.

source_records.source_title: Grok Build Uploaded Entire Git Repositories to xAI Storage, Not Just Files It Read
source_records.author_or_publisher: The Hacker News / reporting on cereblab wire-level testing
source_records.deployment_context: Agentic coding CLI operating against local Git repositories.
source_records.source_context: The report describes wire-level testing in which Grok Build v0.2.93 uploaded complete Git repositories, including commit history and files not required for the active task, through a storage channel distinct from model-turn traffic. The reported transfer volume tracked repository size rather than files inspected by the model.
source_records.relevance_note: Direct evidence that workspace read access and task execution were treated as sufficient authority for repository-scale replication and external transmission.

primary_artefact_access.access_status: public reporting directly reviewed; underlying packet capture not independently reproduced
limitations: The reviewing system did not reproduce the network capture.
Deletion of previously transferred data was not independently verified.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Used for the source-qualified finding that repository-scale upload occurred in the tested version and was subsequently disabled.

source_records.deployment_context: Official product-version record corresponding to the independently tested client version.
source_records.source_context: The official changelog confirms the existence and release timing of Grok Build v0.2.93 but does not itself disclose repository-scale storage upload behaviour.
source_records.relevance_note: Confirms the relevant product version and provides first-party release provenance.

limitations: The changelog is not evidence of the undisclosed transfer behaviour by itself.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Used only to establish first-party product-version provenance.

system_context.system_type: AI coding agent / repository-connected assistant / tool-mediated workspace runtime
system_context.interface_surface: local repository, CLI, model endpoint, storage endpoint, tool and filesystem access
system_context.interaction_mode: repository inspection, coding assistance, file access, remote model inference, and workspace transfer
system_context.deployment_context: Developer workstation and repository-connected coding workflow.
system_context.user_role: developer, repository owner, employee, contractor, security reviewer, or organisation administrator
system_context.affected_population: developers, repository owners, employers, customers, data subjects, and organisations whose code or records are present in connected workspaces

jurisdictional_context.sector: AI coding assistants / software development / cloud services

failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an AI-enabled system treats permission to access, inspect, analyse, or act within a workspace as implicit permission to copy, bundle, transmit, replicate, persist, index, retain, train on, or otherwise reuse data from that workspace beyond the minimum scope required for the authorised task. The failure includes undisclosed repository-scale transfer, transfer of files not inspected or required, inclusion of deleted history or ignored files, inaccurate representation of privacy controls, and absence of a purpose-bound egress manifest.
failure_threshold: The threshold is met when data materially exceeding the requested or necessary task scope leaves the governed environment; when read authority is silently converted into transmission, persistence, training, or secondary-use authority; when the user cannot determine destination, scope, purpose, retention, or deletion status; or when a privacy or model-improvement control is represented as an egress control despite not preventing transmission.

failure_classification.likelihood: active where repository-connected agents use undisclosed or over-broad storage pathways
failure_classification.confidence: corroborated for the cited Grok Build version; wider prevalence remains unassessed
failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: potentially systematic while transfer is implemented as a default client or server behaviour
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — Security & Integrity / Governance / execution-boundary classification
failure_classification.persistence: active until transfer pathways, historical data disposition, and permission semantics are independently verified
failure_classification.reproducibility: network interception, controlled repository-size testing, canary files, endpoint logging, and storage-transfer comparison
failure_classification.visibility: network traffic, storage manifests, unexpected repository bundling, policy-control mismatch, and unexplained remote retention

triage.triage_owner: VIGIL maintainers with CAM SECURITY, OPERATIONS, provenance, privacy, tool-governance, and sovereign-boundary review
triage.mitigation_status: Reported behaviour was disabled server-side; prior-data deletion and complete governance repair remain unverified.
triage.recommended_next_step: Define separate read, analysis, transmission, replication, persistence, training, secondary-use, and deletion authorities; require purpose-bound egress manifests, minimum-necessary transfer, truthful privacy-control semantics, and independently auditable deletion provenance.

cam_internal.governance_layer: tool authority / workspace custody / data egress / purpose limitation / retention and deletion provenance
cam_internal.proposal_needed: yes — assess a purpose-bound workspace custody and data-egress authority primitive
routing_note: VIGIL records the ecosystem failure; normative repair belongs in Caelestis.
The failure is not limited to malicious exfiltration; undisclosed over-broad transfer is sufficient.
Read authority, transmission authority, persistence authority, training authority, and secondary-use authority must remain distinct.
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: new failure-mode record; generated indexes should rebuild deterministically

repair_status.verification_note: The reported upload pathway was disabled, but deletion of prior data and broader ecosystem conformity have not been independently verified.
remaining_gaps: Explicit separation of read, transmission, replication, persistence, training, secondary-use, and deletion authority.

ecosystem_status.basis: The cited product behaviour was reportedly disabled, but prior-data disposition and cross-vendor prevalence remain unresolved.

corpus_coverage.coverage_summary: Proportional tool invocation, source authority, boundary integrity, provenance, and sovereign-assurance doctrine are adjacent, but explicit separation of workspace read, transmission, persistence, training, and secondary-use authority has not been verified.

review_history.review_scope: Evidence qualification, failure classification, authority decomposition, and preliminary CAM routing.
known_limitations: The network experiment was not independently reproduced.
Previously transferred data deletion was not independently verified.
review_history.review_outcome: A distinct security-integrity failure mode is warranted because workspace visibility and task authority were collapsed into repository-scale transmission authority.

current_ai_review.review_scope: Evidence qualification, failure classification, authority decomposition, and preliminary CAM routing
known_limitations: The network experiment was not independently reproduced.
Previously transferred data deletion was not independently verified.
current_ai_review.review_outcome: New failure mode created with source-qualified claims and explicit authority-boundary distinctions.

interpretive_provenance.operating_model: AI-led analytical observatory with high-level human governance editorship

human_governance_editor.role: Human governance editor and CAM constitutional authority
human_governance_editor.review_level: Directed recognition of the failure and authorised repository action; line-by-line review remains available.
human_governance_editor.authority_boundary: Human editorship governs VIGIL direction and CAM adoption; the AI analytical reviewer performs evidence synthesis, classification, drafting, and repository assistance.

interpretive_provenance.historical_reviewer_note: Current 2026 review; earlier model identities are not retroactively inferred.
